metadata.title: Guideline on clinical evaluation of medicinal products used in weight control (Draft, Revision 1)
metadata.document_type: Draft Guideline — released for public consultation; replaces CPMP/EWP/281/96 Rev.1
metadata.note: This is the second revision of the CPMP Guideline on clinical investigation of medicinal products used in Weight Control (CPMP/EWP/281/96). Released as draft for consultation 31 July 2014, comments deadline 31 January 2015. See also the finalized version (guideline-clinical-evaluation-medicinal-products-used-weight-management_en.pdf) if available.

rules.rule_text: Reduction of body weight must be the primary efficacy endpoint in clinical studies for anti-obesity medicinal products; it should preferably be supported by clinically relevant effects on endpoints reflecting the beneficial effect of the documented weight loss.
rules.verbatim_quote: Reduction of body weight should be the primary efficacy endpoint in the clinical studies. However, it should preferably be supported by clinically relevant effects on endpoints reflecting the beneficial effect of the documented weight loss.

rules.rule_text: Demonstration of a clinically significant degree of weight loss of at least 5–10% of baseline weight, which is also at least 5% greater than that associated with placebo, is considered to be a valid primary efficacy criterion in clinical trials evaluating new anti-obesity drugs.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2. Reduction of body weight and related variables
rules.verbatim_quote: Demonstration of a clinically significant degree of weight loss of at least 5- 10% of baseline weight, which is also at least 5% greater than that associated with placebo, is considered to be a valid primary efficacy criterion in clinical trials evaluating new anti-obesity drugs.
rules.exceptions: Alternative: proportion of responders with >10% weight loss at end of 12-month period may be used as alternative primary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Proportions of responders with ≥5% weight loss must be documented as a secondary endpoint in anti-obesity clinical trials.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2. Reduction of body weight and related variables
rules.verbatim_quote: Proportions of responders with ≥ 5% weight loss should be documented as a secondary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Measurements of central adiposity (e.g. waist circumference or waist to hip ratio) must always be documented in anti-obesity clinical trials.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2. Reduction of body weight and related variables
rules.verbatim_quote: Measurements of central adiposity (e.g. waist circumference or waist to hip ratio) should always be documented.

rules.rule_text: Measurements using accepted and validated methods (DEXA, magnetic resonance imaging, or computer tomography) must demonstrate that weight loss is associated with appropriate loss of body fat, as distinct from muscle or body water.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2. Reduction of body weight and related variables
rules.verbatim_quote: Measurements using accepted and validated methods (i.e. DEXA, magnetic resonance imaging or computer tomography) should demonstrate that weight loss is associated with appropriate loss of body fat (as distinct from muscle or body water).

rules.rule_text: A new weight-lowering agent must in general show a neutral or beneficial effect on parameters associated with cardiovascular risk (e.g. blood glucose, blood pressure, lipid levels); the impact on the risk of development of diabetes is considered an important secondary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: A new weight-lowering agent should in general show a neutral or beneficial effect on parameters associated with cardiovascular risk (e.g. blood glucose, blood pressure, lipid levels). The impact on the risk of the development of diabetes is considered as an important secondary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: For products that have shown clinically relevant weight reductions, there is no requirement to demonstrate a direct positive effect on cardiovascular morbidity or mortality prior to licensing unless specific cardiovascular claims are made.
rules.verbatim_quote: For products that have shown clinically relevant weight reductions, there will be no requirement to demonstrate a direct positive effect on cardiovascular morbidity or mortality prior to licensing unless specific claims are made.
rules.exceptions: If cardiovascular morbidity/mortality claims are made, well-designed clinical trials enrolling a representative 'real world' sample of patients with obesity are required.

rules.rule_text: Obesity diagnosis for patient eligibility in pharmaceutical trials must be based on a BMI of 30 kg/m2 or more in both males and females. For patients with multiple CV risk factors, a lower BMI at baseline may be considered.
rules.verbatim_quote: Obesity should be diagnosed on the basis of a body mass index (BMI) of 30 kg/m2 or more in both males and females. For patients with multiple CV risk factors, a lower BMI at baseline could be considered.
rules.exceptions: Lower BMI threshold may be applied for patients with multiple CV risk factors.

rules.rule_text: Development programmes must always include representative samples of patients with class II (BMI ≥35 kg/m2) and class III obesity (BMI ≥40 kg/m2), given the increasing risk of morbidity/mortality with increasing BMI.
rules.verbatim_quote: Considering that the risk of morbidity and mortality as well as other complications increases with increasing BMI, development programs should always include representative samples of patients with class II (BMI ≥35 kg/m2) and III obesity (BMI ≥40 kg/m2).

rules.rule_text: The mechanism of action of the drug must be established and discussed, and it must be demonstrated that weight loss is associated with appropriate loss of fat in pharmacodynamic/pharmacokinetic studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: The mechanism of action of the drug should be established and discussed. It should be demonstrated that weight loss is associated with appropriate loss of fat.

rules.rule_text: Pharmacokinetic studies must be performed to characterize drug disposition; physiological changes associated with obesity and their effects on distribution, protein binding, metabolism and renal excretion must be considered and investigated if relevant. Relevant drug interactions (e.g. with antihypertensives, glucose-lowering, and lipid-modifying agents) must be considered and investigated.
rules.verbatim_quote: Pharmacokinetic studies should be performed to characterize the disposition of the drug. Physiological changes associated with obesity and their effects on the distribution, protein binding, metabolism and renal excretion of drugs should be considered and investigated if considered relevant. Depending on the drug and its mode of action, relevant interactions (with for example antihypertensives, glucose lowering and lipid modifying agents) should be considered and investigated.

rules.rule_text: Confirmatory phase III trials for anti-obesity medicinal products must be randomised, placebo-controlled, and double-blind.
rules.verbatim_quote: Confirmatory phase III trials should be randomised, placebo controlled and double blind.
rules.exceptions: As long-term studies with effective drugs become available, actively-controlled trial designs may become appropriate in addition to placebo-controlled trials.

rules.rule_text: For studies with duration ≤12 months, a run-in period must be implemented in which all patients receive similar instructions, advice and encouragement regarding diet, behaviour modification, and exercise before randomisation.
rules.verbatim_quote: The need for a weight reducing diet run-in period will depend on the duration of the study. For studies with duration of 12 months or longer, this may not be necessary. For studies with ≤12 month duration, a run-in period in which all patients should be given similar instructions, advice and encouragement with regard to diet and behaviour modification and exercise should be implemented before randomisation.
rules.exceptions: Studies with duration of 12 months or longer may not require a run-in period.

rules.rule_text: At least 6-month duration confirmatory trials are required to establish weight loss effect; at least one pivotal trial with duration of ≥12 months is expected to verify beneficial effect on weight development and obesity-related comorbidities.
rules.verbatim_quote: Weight loss has often been observed to plateau after 5 to 6 months of continuous treatment with currently or previously available treatments, and therefore, at least 6 month duration of confirmatory trials is recommended to establish weight loss effect. However, at least one pivotal trial with duration of ≥ 12 month is expected in order to verify a beneficial effect on weight development and obesity related comorbidities.

rules.rule_text: A randomised withdrawal trial allowing assessment of weight development with and without continued treatment must be considered to support the duration of the weight-lowering effect.
rules.verbatim_quote: To support the duration of the weight lowering effect, a randomised withdrawal trial that allows assessment of weight development with and without continued treatment should be considered.

rules.rule_text: All trials must be designed to ensure that patients have follow-up examinations for a period deemed appropriate to assess withdrawal or rebound effects and the effect of drug cessation on appetite and weight control.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is essential that all trials should be designed to ensure that patients participating in these studies should have follow up examinations for a period deemed appropriate to assess withdrawal or rebound effects and the effect of drug cessation on appetite and weight control.

rules.rule_text: The predictive value of a range of percent weight loss after e.g. 3 months of treatment with respect to long-term weight loss (e.g. after 12 months of treatment) must be presented.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients who fail to respond to treatment should be identified, as successful weight loss in the first months of treatment may predict long term effects. The predictive value of a range of % weight loss after e.g. 3 months treatment with respect to long term weight loss (e.g. after 12 months treatment) should be presented.

rules.rule_text: Non-clinical data in relevant animal models evaluating potential effects on different safety aspects must be conducted and provided as an instrumental element of safety evaluation, in accordance with ICH guidelines S7A and S7B.
rules.verbatim_quote: Non-clinical data in relevant animal models evaluating the potential effect of the test drug on different safety aspects should be conducted and provided as an instrumental element of the safety evaluation as outlined in ICH guidelines (e.g. S7A and S7B).

rules.rule_text: An overall prospective plan for the detection and evaluation of potential adverse events, including justification of study size and duration with respect to the possibility of detecting safety signals, must be designed early during clinical development — optimally by the time of phase II studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the clinical studies, an overall plan for the detection and evaluation of potential adverse events, including justification of the size and duration of the studies with respect to the possibility of detecting safety signals, should be prospectively designed early during the clinical development, optimally by the time of phase II studies.

rules.rule_text: Prospective assessment of depression status and suicidality must be included in the clinical trial programme for all centrally acting anti-obesity agents, especially those acting via serotonin pathways; other psychiatric adverse events (anxiety, insomnia) must also be carefully assessed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Prospective assessment of depression status and suicidality should be included in the clinical trial programme for all centrally acting agents, especially for those acting via serotonin pathways. Other psychiatric adverse events such as anxiety and insomnia should also be carefully assessed.
rules.exceptions: Primarily applies to centrally acting anorectic agents.

rules.rule_text: Well-validated scoring tools must be used to assess neuropsychiatric adverse events in anti-obesity clinical trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: Well-validated scoring tools should be used to assess such neuropsychiatric adverse events.

rules.rule_text: Subjects with a history of mild to moderate depression and those using anti-depressive treatment must not be excluded from anti-obesity clinical trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: To reflect clinical practice, subjects with a history of mild to moderate depression and those using anti-depressive treatment should not be excluded from the trials.

rules.rule_text: For anti-obesity drugs acting through catecholamine pathways, withdrawal effects must be studied specifically; where withdrawal effects are noted, therapeutic manoeuvres to reduce or minimise such effects must be investigated.
rules.verbatim_quote: For these drugs, particular attention should be paid to the potential for drug abuse or dependence; withdrawal effects should be studied specifically. Where withdrawal effects are noted, therapeutic manoeuvres to reduce or minimize such effects should be investigated.
rules.exceptions: Serotonin pathway drugs have lower abuse/dependence potential but must still be justified for each specific drug.

rules.rule_text: The development programme for weight control medicinal products must provide sufficient information to support the lack of excess cardiovascular risk; two approaches are acceptable: (1) a meta-analytic approach with adequate size and mean duration (minimum 12 months), or (2) a dedicated cardiovascular outcome study with sufficient duration and power — the latter is required whenever a cardiovascular safety concern is intrinsic to the molecule or has emerged from pre-clinical or clinical studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Concerning the clinical studies, two approaches are conceivable at the time of submission of the MAA to reliably exclude an increase in cardiovascular risk associated with the drug: 1. A meta-analytic approach with adequate size and mean duration (minimum of 12 months)... 2. A dedicated cardiovascular outcome study with sufficient duration and power. This approach is favored whenever a cardiovascular safety concern is intrinsic in the molecule/mechanism of action or has emerged from pre-clinical or clinical studies and/or results from meta-analyses.
rules.exceptions: A dedicated post-approval cardiovascular outcome study might not be necessary when a meta-analytic approach is used and no increased CV risk is identified.

rules.rule_text: For anti-obesity agents that are 5-HT2B receptor agonists, repeated echocardiography examinations (e.g. at baseline and every 6 months) must be included in a relevant proportion of study participants to assess the risk of valvulopathy. The increased risk of pulmonary arterial hypertension associated with certain centrally acting anorectics must also be addressed in the development programme.
rules.verbatim_quote: Available data support that the mechanism of valvulopathy is attributed to specific agonism for the 5-HT2B receptor (Ref.: Rothman et al.) and therefore agents stimulating this receptor should be carefully assessed with respect to risk of this condition. This evaluation should include repeated echocardiography examinations (e.g. at baseline and every 6 months) in a relevant proportion of study participants. Use of certain centrally acting anorectics has also been associated with an increased risk of pulmonary arterial hypertension. This should be taken into account in the development program.
rules.exceptions: Applies specifically to agents with 5-HT2B agonism and/or centrally acting anorectics.
